scope.goal: freeze the adapter migration at a builder-only, no-owner boundary: Go may resolve config, build adapters, and register boot metadata, while live runtime execution ownership remains in the TypeScript runtime

implemented: froze wave 6 at a governance conclusion instead of extending Go into a runtime owner
confirmed Go ownership stops at resolved-config to adapter-builder to boot metadata registration handoff
confirmed no Go-side global commander, live session orchestrator, or execution control plane is introduced in this lane
confirmed TypeScript runtime remains the owner of tool, hook, permission, and isolated execution boundaries
evidence: backend/cmd/codeflow-server/main.go:135-169 registerConfiguredAgents resolves config, builds adapters, registers idle agent metadata, and closes the transient adapter immediately
backend/internal/commander/commander.go:337-447 BuildAgentFromResolved plus send-options/request-semantics helpers form a builder handoff, not a persistent runtime owner
backend/internal/api/handlers/agents.go:14-22 GetAgents only exposes agent-service snapshots to API consumers
backend/internal/workflow/service.go:119-148 and 295-319 workflow overview/replay load and filter registered agent snapshots without executing agents
packages/core/src/cowork/runtime.ts:395-415 and 814-835 instantiate HeadlessToolRuntime and enforce runtime permission boundaries inside the TypeScript cowork runtime
risk: low: the freeze narrows scope and clarifies ownership, but any future attempt to move live execution into Go must be treated as a new architecture decision rather than a continuation of this wave

notes: Go stops at resolved-config, adapter construction, and boot-time metadata registration
TypeScript runtime keeps HeadlessToolRuntime, hook controls, policy evaluation, and isolated execution decisions
No new global commander singleton or Go-side live execution loop is introduced by this freeze decision
